title: Burgess Dehydration Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Burgess dehydration reaction describing mild syn-elimination of secondary and tertiary alcohols through sulfamate ester formation using the Burgess reagent, with extensions to dehydration of formamides, amides, oximes, and nitroalkanes.
notes: Key concepts: Burgess reagent, sulfamate ester, syn elimination, mild dehydration.

title: Burgess Dehydration Reaction – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Burgess dehydration reaction featuring exocyclic alkene formation in taxol synthesis, oxazole construction in madumycin II synthesis, and enone generation en route to herbicidin B.
notes: Natural product oriented dehydration and cyclodehydration examples.

title: Cannizzaro Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Cannizzaro reaction describing base-promoted disproportionation of non-enolizable aldehydes into alcohol and carboxylate products via hydride transfer, including crossed and intramolecular variants and Lewis-acid-enabled asymmetric versions.
notes: Key concepts: hydride transfer, non-enolizable aldehydes, crossed Cannizzaro, intramolecular Cannizzaro.

title: Cannizzaro Reaction – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Cannizzaro reaction featuring transannular prismane-lactone formation, dibenzoheptalene bislactones, hydroxymethyl pyridine synthesis, and atropoenantioselective coumarin assembly.
notes: Examples emphasize unusual intramolecular and crossed hydride-transfer manifolds.

title: Carroll Rearrangement (Kimel-Cope Rearrangement) – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Carroll rearrangement describing [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allylic beta-keto esters to gamma,delta-unsaturated ketones, often followed by decarboxylation, and conceptually related to a decarboxylative Claisen rearrangement.
notes: Key concepts: ester enolate, [3,3]-sigmatropic shift, decarboxylation, gamma-delta unsaturated ketone formation.

title: Carroll Rearrangement (Kimel-Cope Rearrangement) – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Carroll rearrangement featuring malyngolide, 4-epi-acetomycin, prelog-djerassi lactone, and substituted arylacetones generated through stereoselective ester-enolate or beta-keto ester rearrangement sequences.
notes: Highlights stereochemical control from chair-like transition states.

title: Castro-Stephens Coupling – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Castro-Stephens coupling describing copper(I)-mediated coupling of aryl or vinyl halides with copper acetylides or terminal alkynes to form substituted alkynes and, when ortho-substituents are present, heterocycles.
notes: Key concepts: copper acetylides, aryl/vinyl halides, four-centered transition state, heterocycle formation.

title: Castro-Stephens Coupling – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Castro-Stephens coupling featuring isocumestan synthesis, tribenzocyclotriyne assembly, macrocyclic diene/triene lactones, and epothilone B fragment coupling under copper-mediated alkynylation conditions.
notes: Examples emphasize macrocyclization and polyalkyne assembly.

title: Chichibabin Amination Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Chichibabin amination reaction describing nucleophilic aromatic substitution of hydride on electron-deficient azines and diazines by amide or amine equivalents to install amino groups under strongly basic conditions.
notes: Key concepts: amide ion, heteroaromatic SNAr, hydride loss, electron-deficient azines.

title: Chichibabin Amination Reaction – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Chichibabin amination reaction featuring PHIP mutagen synthesis, tetrahydronaphthyridine preparation, bisubstrate reaction templates, and pyridinophane macrocycle assembly via single or double Chichibabin-type amination.
notes: Examples emphasize aminopyridine synthesis and macrocyclic aza-heterocycle construction.
